config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Society matters a lot, but giving it automatic priority over individual rights is dangerous because it can be used to justify abuse, censorship, and unfair treatment of minorities. In my view, a good society protects people first, then asks individuals to accept limits only when there is a clear public need and strong safeguards. The rights of the individual should not be treated as disposable just because the majority wants something.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan atas hak individu, karena dari sudut pandang saya di Amerika, itu terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Hak individu memang tidak absolut, tetapi justru menjadi pagar supaya mayoritas tidak bisa seenaknya menekan minoritas atas nama "kebaikan bersama". Saya setuju bahwa ada keadaan tertentu, seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan, di mana pembatasan terbatas bisa dibenarkan, tetapi harus sempit, transparan, dan bisa diuji. Kalau tidak, frasa itu hanya jadi alasan halus untuk mengorbankan kebebasan dasar orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the individual, because in Indonesia that idea can too easily become an excuse for silence, conformity, and abuse of power. We do value gotong royong and social harmony, but I have also seen how “for the public good” can be used to pressure minorities or dismiss personal dignity. The better principle is that individual rights set the boundaries, and society can only limit them in narrow, clearly justified cases like public safety or health. Without that limit, “society first” becomes a slogan that benefits the powerful more than the community.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena di Amerika saya melihat betapa mudahnya “kepentingan masyarakat” dipakai untuk menekan kebebasan berbicara, hak minoritas, dan perlindungan hukum individu. Kepentingan publik memang nyata, tetapi justru harus dibatasi oleh hak individu agar mayoritas atau pemerintah tidak bisa bertindak semaunya atas nama kebaikan bersama. Saya bisa menerima pembatasan yang sempit dan benar-benar perlu, seperti untuk kesehatan publik atau keselamatan, tetapi itu harus punya alasan yang jelas dan pengawasan ketat. Kalau tidak, prioritas pada masyarakat sering kali bukan melindungi semua orang, melainkan memberi kuasa lebih besar kepada pihak yang sudah kuat.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the idea that society should generally take priority over individual rights, because in Indonesia that often ends up meaning the loudest group gets to decide what is “good” for everyone else. We do value togetherness and public order, but I have seen how appeals to harmony can silence dissent, pressure minorities, and excuse unfair treatment. Individual rights should be the baseline, and society should only override them in limited cases like genuine public safety or health needs, with clear rules and accountability. Without that, “the interests of society” becomes too easy a cover for power, not justice.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena dari sudut pandang saya di Amerika, memberi prioritas otomatis pada kepentingan masyarakat terlalu mudah berubah menjadi pembenaran untuk menekan kebebasan berbicara, hak minoritas, dan proses hukum yang adil. Saya setuju bahwa masyarakat punya kepentingan nyata, tetapi kepentingan itu harus dibatasi oleh hak individu supaya mayoritas atau pemerintah tidak bisa bertindak semaunya atas nama kebaikan bersama. Pembatasan memang bisa dibenarkan dalam keadaan tertentu seperti kesehatan publik atau keselamatan, tetapi harus sempit, transparan, dan diawasi ketat. Tanpa itu, slogan “masyarakat di atas individu” lebih sering menguntungkan pihak yang sudah kuat daripada melindungi semua orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
